rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFIORNIA VS. PROPERTY SUBJECT TO DISPOSITION: $1,023.00 U.S. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Petitioner'S First Set Of Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents; Petitioner'S First Set Of Special Interrogatories; Judicial Council Form Interrogatories [Proposed Order]
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, June 11, 2014, Line 1, PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFIORNIA's Motion To Compel Answers To Petitioner's First Set Of Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents; Petitioner's First Set Of Special Interrogatories; Judicial Council Form Interrogatories. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, for the reasons set forth in Petitioner People of the State of California's motion, which is unopposed. Claimant Daniel Faulalo is ordered to serve complete verified responses, without objection, and to produce documents to Petitioner's Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents (Set One), Form Interrogatories (Set One), and Special Interrogatories (Set One) within fifteen (15) days of the date of notice of the written Order on this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: EQUABLE ASCENT FINANCIAL, LLC VS. PATRICK RODDIE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Strike And Tax Memo Of Costs; Ntc Of Non-Appearance
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wenesday, June 11, 2014, Line 5, PLAINTIFF EQUABLE ASCENT FINANCIAL, LLC's motion To Strike And Tax Memo Of Costs; Ntc Of Non-Appearance Granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiff's motion to strike costs is granted as to the $1,000 in attorney's fees sought pursuant to Civ. Code sec. 1717.5. Civ. Code sec. 1717.5 explicitly requires that a party be found to have no obligation owing on a book account in order to be awarded reasonable attorney's fees as a prevailing party. Because Plaintiff dismissed the complaint prior to trial without prejudice and no pre-trial motions were filed, no findings have been made on whether Defendant had an obligation owing on a book account. Plaintiff's motion to strike costs is denied as to all other costs. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/eg

rulings.Case Title: SCULPT GARDENS INC., A CORPORATION VS. MARK MARROCCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wenesday, June 11, 2014, Line 6, DEFENDANTS MARK MARROCCO AND HEATHER MARROCCO'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Overruled. The written contract attached as Exhibit A to the Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") states that the $28,850 was an estimate, and the parties would be subsequently billed bi-weekly for work actually performed. Additionally, the SAC alleges that Plaintiff performed "extra work pursuant to change orders." (SAC, par. 7.) This is sufficient to state a cause of action for breach of the written contract. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: KAYLEE ANN ULLOM VS. ANOTHER PLANET ENTERTAINMENT, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wenesday, June 11, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANT OSL GROUP, LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Denied. Punitive damages may be awarded in negligence cases if the plaintiff can establish that the defendant engaged in "despicable conduct¿with a willful and conscious disregard of the rights or safety of others." (Civ. Code sec. 3294(c)(1).) The Second Amended Complaint pleads that Defendant knew that criminal activity, including sexual assaults, was occurring in the wooded area during the festival, but Defendant took no additional precautions to protect its patrons. This is sufficient to support a claim for punitive damages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/eg

rulings.Case Title: PRECISION INDUSTRIAL CONSTRUCTORS, INC. VS. SNOWFLAKE MILL INVESTORS, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wenesday, June 11, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANTS SNOWFLAKE MILL INVESTORS, LLC, RABIN WORLDWIDE, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION AND RYAN SMITH'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT The demurrer to the declaratory relief cause of action is sustained without leave to amend under CCP 1061. The complaint complains about past wrongs and not a present controversy between the parties. Plaintiff can redress the alleged wrongs through its other causes of action. The demurrer to the injunctive relief cause of action is sustained with leave to amend to plead that claim as a remedy/prayer for relief. The demurrers to the two interference causes of action are sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to allege distinctly interference with contract and/or interference with prospective advantage causes of action. The demurrers to the remaining causes of action are overruled. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges facts to support those claims and defendant raises factual issues that cannot be resolved on demurrer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: SYSCO SAN FRANCISCO INC., VS. KIEN LE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wenesday, June 11, 2014, Line 14, PLAINTIFF SYSCO SAN FRANCISCO INCORPORATED'S APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER Denied. Plaintiff Sysco San Francisco applies for a writ of attachment against Defendant Amir Asli, individually and dba Bacheeso and dba Bacheeso Garden Bistro. (Application, 2.) Plaintiff has not established the probable validity of its claim against Mr. Asli. (CCP § 484.090.) The open book account identifies the account holder as "Bacheeso", and not Mr. Asli. (Ableman Decl., Exhibit A.) Plaintiff has not presented sufficient evidence to demonstrate that Mr. Asli does business as Bacheeso. In the Complaint, Plaintiff alleges that Mr. Asli signed a personal guaranty. (Complaint, 14, Exhibit B.) However, the Complaint is not verified as averred in the Application. (Compare Complaint; with Application, 7a, Cook Decl., p. 1:10-11.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/EG
